name: Spike.sh Incident Alerting API
what_it_does: Incident alerting and on-call management platform with REST API — providing alert routing, on-call scheduling, escalation policies, and status pages as an affordable alternative to PagerDuty, with phone call, SMS, Slack, and email alerts.
use_cases: Routing monitoring alerts to on-call engineers via API-triggered incidents
Managing on-call schedules and escalation policies programmatically
Creating incidents from external monitoring or CI/CD systems via REST API
Querying incident history and resolution times for reliability reporting
Managing status page incidents and maintenance windows via API
not_for: Teams with complex SRE workflows requiring postmortem automation (use Rootly)
Enterprise reliability teams requiring formal SLA commitments
Organizations needing deep Jira/ServiceNow integration for ITSM workflows
best_when: A small to medium engineering team needs simple, affordable on-call alerting with phone calls and SMS as PagerDuty alternative without enterprise pricing.
avoid_when: You need enterprise SRE features, complex postmortem workflows, or are deeply integrated with enterprise ITSM systems.

auth.notes: API key from Spike.sh dashboard. Bearer token authentication. Account-scoped access. Integration tokens for inbound webhooks from monitoring tools. Separate tokens per integration.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $5/user/month; Growth from $10; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Very affordable vs. PagerDuty ($21+/user/month). Phone calls included in paid plans. Growing startup with active development. Good for bootstrapped teams and startups needing on-call without enterprise cost.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; not prominently documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers incidents, escalation policies, on-call schedules, and integrations. Webhook support for inbound alerts. Smaller API surface than enterprise tools. Documentation improving with product maturity.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Incident creation creates new incidents. Alert deduplication available via integration key. On-call schedule queries are idempotent reads. Update operations idempotent for same values.
known_agent_gotchas: Smaller API surface than PagerDuty — some advanced operations require UI
Integration-specific webhook URLs change if integration is deleted and recreated
Phone call alerts use third-party providers — verify reliability for critical workflows
Younger product — API may evolve; pin to documented endpoint versions

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth. GDPR compliant. US data residency. Growing startup — formal security certifications still in progress. Integration token security for inbound webhooks.
